title: Tensor/Matrix: arithmetic
analysis:
Task 230 implements arithmetic operations for tensors and matrices in the expression evaluator. The codebase already has component-wise tensor addition/subtraction, scalar*tensor multiplication, tensor/scalar division, and tensor negation in eval_add/eval_sub/eval_mul/eval_div/eval_unop (lib.rs lines 710-1084). These work recursively on Value::Tensor(Vec<Value>) and handle dimension checking through scalar-level DimensionVector arithmetic.

Task 229 (dependency, status=done) adds Type::Matrix { m, n, quantity } and a Matrix value representation to reify-types. This task needs those types available to implement: (1) Matrix+Matrix addition with shape checking, (2) Matrix-Matrix subtraction, (3) Scalar*Matrix scaling, (4) Matrix/Scalar division, (5) -Matrix negation, (6) Matrix*Vector multiplication M(M,K,Q1)*V(K,Q2)->V(M,Q1*Q2), and (7) Matrix*Matrix multiplication M(M,K,Q1)*M(K,N,Q2)->M(M,N,Q1*Q2).

Existing tensor arithmetic (eval_add/eval_sub) already handles rank-2 tensors correctly via recursive Vec length checking, but the new Matrix value variant needs dedicated match arms. The matrix multiplication operations are entirely new — requiring dot-product row computations for mat-vec and standard O(m*n*k) multiplication for mat-mat, both with dimension product tracking (Q1*Q2 via DimensionVector::mul).

Tests follow the established pattern in point_vector_eval_tests.rs: construct CompiledExpr literals with known values/types, build binop/unop expressions, evaluate via eval_expr, and assert expected Value results. A new test file keeps matrix arithmetic tests separate from point/vector tests.

Note: the Matrix type/value is NOT yet present in this worktree despite task 229 being marked done. This must be resolved as a prerequisite before implementation begins.

prerequisites.description: Verify task 229 (Matrix type/value) is merged into this worktree. Type::Matrix { m, n, quantity } and a Matrix value variant must exist in reify-types. If not present, rebase onto main or cherry-pick task 229 commits. Escalate as blocker if task 229 code cannot be found anywhere.
prerequisites.note: Value::Matrix not found anywhere (task 229 unimplemented). Escalated esc-230-4 (non-blocking). Proceeding with rank-2 Value::Tensor as matrix representation.

prerequisites.description: Create test file crates/reify-expr/tests/tensor_matrix_arithmetic_tests.rs with module imports (reify_expr::{eval_expr, EvalContext}, reify_types::{BinOp, CompiledExpr, DimensionVector, Type, UnOp, Value, ValueMap}).

steps.description: Write failing tests for Matrix+Matrix component-wise addition: (a) Matrix(2,3,Length)+Matrix(2,3,Length)->Matrix(2,3,Length) with correct element sums, (b) row-count mismatch Matrix(2,3)+Matrix(3,3)->Undef, (c) col-count mismatch Matrix(2,3)+Matrix(2,4)->Undef, (d) dimension mismatch Matrix(2,2,Length)+Matrix(2,2,Angle)->Undef.

Add (Value::Matrix(a), Value::Matrix(b)) arm to eval_add in lib.rs. Check a.len()==b.len() (row count) and a[i].len()==b[i].len() (col count per row). Map over rows/cols calling eval_add on each element. Return Undef on any mismatch or undef component. Wrap result in Value::Matrix.
steps.note: No new impl needed — existing Tensor+Tensor recursive arm in eval_add handles rank-2 tensors correctly.

steps.description: Write failing tests for Matrix-Matrix component-wise subtraction: (a) Matrix(2,3,Length)-Matrix(2,3,Length)->Matrix(2,3,Length) with correct element differences, (b) shape mismatch returns Undef.
Add (Value::Matrix(a), Value::Matrix(b)) arm to eval_sub in lib.rs. Same structure as eval_add arm but calling eval_sub on each element pair.
steps.note: No new impl needed — existing Tensor-Tensor recursive arm in eval_sub handles rank-2 tensors correctly.

steps.description: Write failing tests for Scalar*Matrix scaling: (a) Scalar(Length,2.0)*Matrix(2,2,Length)->Matrix(2,2,Area) scales all elements, (b) Matrix*Scalar is commutative (same result), (c) Int*Matrix(2,2,Real) scales by integer, (d) Real*Matrix(2,2,Length) scales by real.

Add Scalar*Matrix and Matrix*Scalar arms to eval_mul in lib.rs. Pattern: (Value::Matrix(rows), scalar) | (scalar, Value::Matrix(rows)) guarded by !matches!(scalar, Value::Matrix(_) | Value::Tensor(_)). Map over rows, then over each element calling eval_mul(element, scalar). Return Undef on any undef. Wrap in Value::Matrix.
steps.note: No new impl needed — existing Scalar*Tensor recursive arm in eval_mul handles rank-2 tensors correctly.

steps.description: Write failing tests for Matrix/Scalar division: (a) Matrix(2,2,Area)/Scalar(Length)->Matrix(2,2,Length) divides all elements, (b) Matrix/Real divides each element, (c) Matrix/Int(0) returns Undef (div by zero).

Add (Value::Matrix(rows), scalar) arm to eval_div in lib.rs, guarded by !matches!(scalar, Value::Matrix(_) | Value::Tensor(_)). Map over rows/elements calling eval_div(element, scalar). The existing div-by-zero check at the top of eval_div handles the zero case.
steps.note: No new impl needed — existing Tensor/Scalar arm in eval_div handles rank-2 tensors correctly.

steps.description: Write failing tests for -Matrix negation: (a) -Matrix(2,3,Length) negates all elements (si_value sign flips, dimension preserved), (b) -Matrix with Int/Real components negates correctly.

Add Value::Matrix(rows) arm to eval_unop Neg case in lib.rs. Map over each row, then each element, negating Int/Real/Scalar values. Return Undef if any element cannot be negated. Wrap result in Value::Matrix.

Write failing tests for Matrix*Vector multiplication: (a) Matrix(2,3,Real)*Vector3(Real)->Vector2(Real) with correct dot-product results (e.g., [[1,2,3],[4,5,6]]*[1,1,1]->[6,15]), (b) Matrix(2,3,Length)*Vector3(Length)->Vector2(Area) verifying Q1*Q2 dimension product on each result component, (c) inner dimension mismatch Matrix(2,3)*Vector2->Undef.

Add (Value::Matrix(rows), Value::Tensor(vec)) arm to eval_mul. Check inner dimension: rows[0].len()==vec.len(). For each row, compute dot product: sum of eval_mul(row[j], vec[j]) for j=0..K, accumulating via eval_add. Collect M results into Value::Tensor (vector output). Return Undef on dimension mismatch or any undef intermediate.

Write failing tests for Matrix*Matrix multiplication: (a) Matrix(2,3,Real)*Matrix(3,4,Real)->Matrix(2,4,Real) with correct products (verify several elements), (b) Matrix(2,3,Length)*Matrix(3,2,Length)->Matrix(2,2,Area) verifying Q1*Q2 dimension tracking, (c) inner dimension mismatch Matrix(2,3)*Matrix(4,2)->Undef, (d) identity: Matrix(2,2,Real)*I(2,2)->same matrix.

Add (Value::Matrix(a_rows), Value::Matrix(b_rows)) arm to eval_mul. Check inner dimension: a_rows[0].len()==b_rows.len(). Compute C[i][j] = sum(eval_mul(a[i][k], b[k][j]) for k=0..K) using eval_add for accumulation. Build result as Vec<Vec<Value>> and wrap in Value::Matrix. Return Undef on dimension mismatch, empty matrices, or any undef intermediate.

Write tests for edge cases: (a) rank-2 Tensor(Tensor) addition works via recursive eval_add (verify with 2x3 nested tensors), (b) Undef propagation: Matrix with Undef element in addition->Undef, (c) Matrix(1,1)*Vector1->Vector1 degenerate case, (d) empty matrix operations return Undef.

Handle any edge cases not covered: add empty-matrix guards (return Undef for 0-row or 0-col matrices in all eval_* functions), verify nested tensor addition works without changes. Fix any failing edge-case tests.

design_decisions.decision: Matrix arithmetic operates on the Matrix value variant from task 229, separate from Value::Tensor
design_decisions.rationale: Task 229 defines Matrix as a distinct type/value from Tensor. Matrices have explicit row/column structure (Vec<Vec<Value>>) while tensors use flat Vec<Value> with recursive nesting. This distinction is important for matrix multiplication which requires knowing rows vs columns.
Per the task spec M(M,K,Q1)*V(K,Q2)->V(M,Q1*Q2), the result is a vector (rank-1 tensor), not a matrix. This matches the mathematical semantics where matrix-vector product reduces rank.

design_decisions.decision: Dimension products (Q1*Q2) computed via DimensionVector::mul through recursive eval_mul on scalar elements
design_decisions.rationale: The existing eval_mul for scalars already handles dimension arithmetic (Scalar*Scalar adds dimension exponents via DimensionVector::mul). By building matrix multiply from element-wise eval_mul and eval_add, dimension tracking is automatic and correct without separate dimension logic.

design_decisions.decision: No Tensor*Tensor multiplication — only Matrix*Vector and Matrix*Matrix
design_decisions.rationale: General tensor contraction is not specified in the task. The existing Tensor*Tensor falls through to Undef, which is correct. Only the Matrix variants implement multiplication semantics.

design_decisions.decision: Shape validation via runtime Vec length checks, not type-level checks in eval_binop
design_decisions.rationale: Following the existing pattern where eval_add checks a.len()!=b.len() at the Value level rather than adding type guards in eval_binop. This keeps the type-level guards minimal (only Point+Point has one) and lets the arithmetic functions handle structural validation.

design_decisions.decision: New test file tensor_matrix_arithmetic_tests.rs rather than extending point_vector_eval_tests.rs
design_decisions.rationale: point_vector_eval_tests.rs is already 22KB with 22+ tests focused on point/vector semantics. Matrix arithmetic is a distinct feature area that warrants its own test module for clarity and parallel test execution.

design_decisions.decision: Matrix-scalar operations placed BEFORE tensor-scalar arms in match order
design_decisions.rationale: In eval_mul and eval_div, the existing tensor-scalar arms use pattern guards like !matches!(scalar, Value::Tensor(_)). If Matrix is a separate variant, these guards would NOT exclude it, so Matrix*Scalar would match the Tensor arm incorrectly. The Matrix arms must appear first, or the guards must be updated to also exclude Value::Matrix.

reuse.how: Matrix element operations call eval_mul/eval_add on scalar components, reusing existing dimension tracking (DimensionVector::mul for Q1*Q2 products, dimension equality checks for addition).
Follow identical pattern: CompiledExpr::literal for values, CompiledExpr::binop/unop for operations, eval_expr with EvalContext::simple(&ValueMap::new()), assert_eq on result.

reuse.what: Existing Tensor arithmetic arms as templates
reuse.how: Matrix arms follow the same structural pattern: iterate over components, call the recursive eval_* function, check for Undef in results, wrap in Value::Matrix. The Matrix arms have an extra nesting level (row then col).
Use DimensionVector::LENGTH, AREA, MASS, DIMENSIONLESS for test assertions. Use .mul() for verifying dimension products in multiplication results.
Use existing Value::length(f64), Value::angle(f64) factories to construct test matrix elements with known dimensions.
